message:
Add IoPhaseOutcome enum and follower-aware backoff deadline selection

Replace the boolean return from drive_io_phase() with a three-state
IoPhaseOutcome enum (Progress, Follower, NoProgress) that captures
whether the worker made I/O progress, was a follower to an active
reactor leader, or found nothing. The new select_backoff_deadline()
function uses this state to prevent follower workers from arming
timeout wakeups on shared global/timer deadlines — followers now only
honor their local deadlines, reducing unnecessary wakeups under
contention when the leader is already responsible for shared deadline
handling.

- three_lane.rs: +99/-9 lines — IoPhaseOutcome enum, select_backoff_deadline()
  helper, drive_io_phase() returns IoPhaseOutcome, backoff logic uses
  follower-aware deadline selection, 3 unit tests covering follower-only-local,
  follower-none, and non-follower-earliest-deadline behaviors

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
